title: Conjecture 1 (Bang-Jensen et al.)
statement_text: For every digraph $F$, the $F$-\textsc{Subdivision} problem is polynomial-time solvable or NP-complete.
context_text: Bang-Jensen et al. conjectured a full dichotomy for the $F$-Subdivision problem. The paper describes this as the central motivating conjecture and provides further support by classifying all 4-vertex digraphs $F$ (up to five exceptions) as tractable or intractable.
paper_contribution: Classifies all digraphs $F$ of order 4 as polynomial-time solvable or NP-complete, up to five exceptions $O_i$, $1 \leq i \leq 5$, providing strong evidence for the dichotomy in this case.
notes: The labeled header reads 'Conjecture 1' without an inline parenthetical, but the immediately preceding sentence explicitly attributes the conjecture to Bang-Jensen et al. [2].
paper_title: Finding a subdivision of a prescribed digraph of order 4

title: Conjecture 5 (Bang-Jensen et al.)
statement_text: $F$-\textsc{Subdivision} is NP-complete for every non-planar digraph $F$.
context_text: Proposed by Bang-Jensen et al. as a counterpart to the tractability result for planar digraphs with no big vertices (Corollary 4), this conjecture predicts that non-planarity of $F$ is a sufficient condition for NP-completeness of $F$-Subdivision.
paper_contribution: Confirms the conjecture for all non-planar 4-vertex digraphs except possibly the five open cases $O_i$, $1 \leq i \leq 5$, since every other non-planar digraph of order 4 is shown to be intractable.
paper_title: Finding a subdivision of a prescribed digraph of order 4

title: Complexity of W3-Subdivision (Bang-Jensen et al.)
statement_text: Is $W_3$-\textsc{Subdivision} polynomial-time solvable or NP-complete, where $W_3$ is the wheel digraph obtained from the directed 3-cycle $\vec{C}_3$ by adding a dominating centre vertex?
context_text: Bang-Jensen et al. proved that $W_2$-Subdivision is tractable and that $W_k$-Subdivision is NP-complete for all $k \geq 4$, explicitly leaving the case $k = 3$ as an open problem. This paper resolves it as a by-product of the 4-vertex classification.
paper_contribution: Resolves the open problem: Theorem 6 implies $W_3$-Subdivision is polynomial-time solvable, since $W_3$ is an oriented graph of order 4 and all oriented graphs of order 4 are shown to be tractable.
notes: No labeled Problem environment in the source paper; open status is asserted in prose: 'The case of W3 was left as an open problem.'
paper_title: Finding a subdivision of a prescribed digraph of order 4
